Ad Text:
The role

Queensland Health recognises that progressing our digital health strategy is an essential part of our ongoing commitment to delivering safe, effective, and accessible healthcare to all Queenslanders.

Implementing an integrated electronic Medical Record (ieMR) across all public hospitals in Queensland is at the heart of our digital transformation and is essential to delivering care, closer to home.  Already used in numerous facilities today, the ieMR is improving care and patient outcomes by offering Queensland Health clinicians access to real-time clinical information for their patients, at point of care.

Queensland Health is currently undertaking several of the largest health ICT programs and projects ever seen in Australia. The largest of these, the Digital Hospital Program (DHP) is a visionary program that continues to enable clinical services and enhance our technological capability by extending the availability of the ieMR into more hospitals and community care venues. The Digital Hospital Program will deliver advanced ieMR to 5 Metro North Hospitals over the first 5 years. In parallel Queensland Health will be adding over 2,811 additional hospital beds across the state, with four entirely new hospitals and expansions to 11 already world-class facilities.

This program represents an opportunity for you to make your mark on the future of healthcare and help to shape the future of health centres across Queensland, for Queenslanders for generations to come. 

As a Principal Business Analyst, you will play a key role in leading and managing the analysis, documentation, and evaluation of both current and future state business processes, services, and roles within a complex digital system in a clinical healthcare environment. You will lead the establishment of new services, ensuring a smooth transition to business-as-usual operations.

Is this the opportunity for you?

To be successful in this role, you must have:

Experience in leading and managing business analysis activities within IM/ICT projects preferably in the health care industry.
Proven experience leading business process modelling activities for large organisations, preferably using Business Process Modelling Notation (BPMN).
High level of knowledge of and the ability to provide advice on the application of appropriate business analysis techniques, methodologies, and tools preferably aligned with Business Analysis Body of Knowledge Version 3.0 (BABOK) is essential.
Ability to lead, manage and mentor a multi-disciplinary team, developing effective working relationships with team members and stakeholders including mentoring other business analysts within the organisation.

The following experience will be highly regarded:

Demonstrated knowledge and prior experience working within an ieMR environment, with a proven track record of leading large-scale, complex digital system implementations in a clinical healthcare setting.

While experience in health care is not a mandatory requirement, we highly value candidates who have demonstrated relevant experience in clinical systems, health IT, or digital health. If your experience is in other sectors, please make sure to outline how your knowledge can transfer to the healthcare context.

To be considered for this exciting opportunity submit your CV or resume and a short statement (maximum 2 pages) addressing your experience in each of the key areas outlined above.

About eHealth Queensland:

eHealth Queensland is one of the largest Information Communication Technology operations in the state. It is responsible for ensuring the smooth operation of information systems and technologies so that Queensland Health employees and health providers have access to information that supports the delivery of health care.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development

To apply for this exciting opportunity, submit your resume and a short statement (maximum 2 pages) on how your experience, abilities, knowledge and personal qualities are relevant for the role, taking into account the key responsibilities and attributes noted in the ‘How you will be assessed' section of the role description.

The Opportunity

This is a hands-on contract opportunity for a Data Analyst to support a major HR and Payroll transformation program at a critical stage of delivery. You will step into a fast-moving environment where testing, data migration, and production readiness are already underway, and the business needs someone who can bring structure, rigour, and execution across complex people and payroll data. The role suits someone who is not just technically strong in ETL and SQL, but who also understands the operational reality of HR/Payroll data, data quality risk, and the discipline required to get legacy and target systems aligned before go-live.


About the Role

In this role, you will take ownership of key data activities across the project lifecycle, helping ensure accurate, complete, and production-ready HR/Payroll data.

Lead data profiling, cleansing, validation, and remediation planning across legacy and target datasets
Support end-to-end ETL activities, including extraction, transformation, loading, optimisation, and troubleshooting
Develop and manage delta catch-up processes to keep source and target systems aligned before go-live
Define and implement data quality rules, KPIs, and governance controls across the HR/Payroll domain
Support testing, cutover, go-live, and post-implementation data readiness activities
Investigate and resolve data issues impacting reporting, system behaviour, and overall data quality


About You

You are a technically strong Data Analyst who understands that bad HR/Payroll data breaks projects, creates payroll risk, and drags out delivery. You know how to work across messy legacy environments, challenge poor data standards, and get things into shape without needing constant direction.

Proven experience in data analysis roles delivering end-to-end ETL and transition-to-BAU activities
Advanced SQL and hands-on SSIS capability across complex datasets and multi-system environments
Strong experience in data quality improvement, data governance, and master data management
Solid background working with HCM/Payroll platforms, with exposure to tools such as SAP, Kronos, Aurion, Workday, or Employee Central Payroll (Aurion & Workday preferred)
Ability to operate effectively in fast-paced project environments with competing deadlines
Relevant qualifications in Data Analytics, Information Management, or a related discipline will be well regarded




The Benefits
This opportunity offers the chance to work on meaningful transformation work with immediate impact.

Initial 3-month contract in a large-scale enterprise transformation environment
Hybrid working model with 3 days onsite and 2 days from home
High-impact role across data migration, governance, testing, and go-live support


How to Apply

Please apply with current resume in Microsoft Word format only (.doc or .docx). If you would like to have a confidential discussion, please contact Nina Magill on nina.magill@Davidonwp.com, quoting reference JN -042026-42943. Want to know more about Davidson? Visit us at www.davidsonwp.com

The Intelligence and Situational Awareness Lead interprets meteorological, hazard, and impact data to enhance situational awareness and support informed decision-making. The role anticipates risks, supports preparedness, and enables coordinated responses to severe weather and multi-hazard events. By transforming complex intelligence into actionable insights, the position strengthens the organisation’s ability to assist customers, partners, and communities through effective disaster response, logistics, and claims management.

 About the role

Maintain situational awareness of severe weather and hazards using meteorological, geospatial, and operational intelligence sources.

Analyse meteorological hazards, severe weather patterns, and climate drivers to support predictive intelligence and operational readiness.

Monitor severe weather systems and assess potential impacts on customers, communities, and business operations, providing actionable insights.

Brief senior decision-makers and support business planning, risk modelling, and hazard outlook development through intelligence analysis.

Leverage social media, open-source intelligence (OSINT), and geospatial tools to enhance real-time situational awareness and communicate hazard intelligence effectively.

Support actuaries and reinsurers with post-event analysis, collaborating with cross-functional teams to assess hazard impacts and operational risks.

About you

Demonstrated qualifications and experience in meteorology with experience in weather management.

 Experience in hazard intelligence or emergency services is beneficial.

 Strong analytical and decision-support skills.

Proficient in geospatial platforms, data visualization tools, and OSINT analysis.

Skilled in operating effectively in high-pressure, data-rich environments.

Demonstrated technical proficiency with systems, software, databases, and reporting tools.

Excellent verbal and written communication skills for clear and impactful information delivery.

A passion for leveraging emerging technologies to stay ahead in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

Work-life balance: Enjoy our flexible, hybrid work arrangements, and tailored workplace adjustments, where possible.

Career Development: Access mentoring, development and global mobility opportunities for growth and success, including access to over 10,000 learning resources.

Ad Text:
Company description:



Department of Energy, Environment and Climate Action



Job description:



The Principal Energy Market Analyst in the Energy Market Intelligence Unit (EMIU) prepares relevant quantitative analysis and modelling to meet the needs of the organisation. This role leads quantitative analysis on energy markets and undertakes energy market modelling using optimisation tools. The position requires a detailed understanding of energy markets. The Principal Market Analyst is responsible for applying and integrating relevant mathematical and statistical processes, using a variety of software, to build electricity, gas and stochastic simulation models. The Principal Market Modelling Analyst is responsible for applying and integrating relevant mathematical and statistical processes, using a variety of software, to build electricity, gas and stochastic simulation models. The position is also responsible for communicating quantitative assumptions, methodologies and insights in clear and concrete terms.

Specialist/Technical Expertise/Qualifications

Bachelor, honours, or advanced degree such as data science, statistics, mathematics or other highly quantitative degree
Demonstrated in-depth understanding of energy market dynamics
High-level familiarity with energy market modelling software such PLEXOS or Prophet
Expert ability in a programming language used in the development of quantitative analysis, machine learning models and energy market models (e.g., R or Python)

This is a fixed term position available for a period of 3 years. The work location for this position is 8 Nicholson Street, East Melbourne with hybrid work arrangements available.

Job Title: Specialist Data Management | Perth | Mon - Fri
Ad Text:
BHP is a world-leading resources company. We work in more than 90 locations worldwide and our products are sold globally.

We've positioned our business to support the megatrends shaping our world. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global infrastructure and the energy transition. Copper for renewable energy. And we are moving into potash to support more sustainable farming.

A resource mix for today - and critical to the future.



About the Role

The Geoscience Systems and Data Management team is seeking a motivated and collaborative Specialist Data Management professional to join WAIO Geoscience.

Come and be a part of a diverse, inclusive, and technical team working towards our purpose of "delivering orebody knowledge to realise our full potential". We enable high‑quality, trusted and accessible geoscience data to characterise Western Australian Iron Ore (WAIO) mineral inventory across both strategic and tactical time horizons for our customers. The successful candidate will play a critical role in ensuring the quality, integrity, security, and availability of WAIO Geoscience data, supporting Geoscientists to extract maximum value from drilling and field data.

Reporting to the Superintendent Geoscience Systems and Data Management, this G10 role forms part of a highly experienced team of geoscience data and systems specialists.

You will be responsible for managing WAIO's critical geoscience datasets including drillhole and field mapping data across exploration, tactical drilling, RCGC, geotechnical, hydrogeological, geometallurgical and closure planning programs and enabling their effective integration into downstream modelling, evaluation, and decision‑making processes.

This role offers the opportunity to deepen your technical expertise across geoscience systems, SQL‑based data platforms, data governance, automation, and cloud technologies, while contributing to continuous improvement and change initiatives across the broader Resource Characterisation function.

About the role

As a key member of the Geoscience Systems and Data Management team within WAIO | Resource Characterisation, you will:

Administer and maintain Geosciences' centralised drilling database, ensuring system reliability, performance, and availability
Perform data loading, validation, transformation, and integrity checks across the drillhole data lifecycle
Manage and support Geoscience data quality processes in alignment with defined data standards and Data Steward requirements
Provide subject matter expertise and support to Geoscience teams for the planning, collection, management and use of drilling and field mapping data
Deliver help‑desk support and advanced troubleshooting for Geoscience systems and data management issues
Develop, maintain, and optimise SQL queries, stored procedures and database objects to support reporting, analytics, and operational workflows
Contribute to Geoscience systems and data initiatives, including platform upgrades, process improvements, and automation opportunities
Support the design and improvement of data governance, documentation, and operational procedures
Identify and implement opportunities to improve efficiency, scalability and data value through automation and database optimisation across on‑premise and cloud environments
Engage and Collaborate with Geoscience and Technology business stakeholders to deliver practical, fit‑for‑purpose data solutions aligned to business strategy
Incorporate product ownership and project management towards Geosciences' centralised drilling database evolving requirements
About You

We're looking for someone who brings a balance of strong technical capability, geoscience domain understanding and customer focus, with a passion for high-quality data and its potential applications. You will ideally have:

Qualifications & Experience

A degree in either Geoscience, Information Management, Computer Science, or a related discipline
Minimum 5 years' experience in a similar data management or database administration role
Experience in exploration and/or mining industry, particularly geoscience or drilling data, is highly regarded

Technical Capability

Design and build database structures, tables, views, stored procedures, optimised for performance and scalability
Hands‑on experience administering databases using Microsoft SQL Server, proficiency in Transact-SQL (T-SQL) is highly desirable
Experience with geological drilling database systems (e.g. Geobank, Acquire)
Exposure to SQL‑based reporting and analytics, such as SSRS and/or Power BI
Working knowledge of cloud or hybrid data platforms (e.g. Snowflake, Databricks) is highly desirable
Experience with SQL and/or Python based application development or data‑driven tools is a strong advantage
Basic to intermediate scripting or programming skills (e.g. Python) are beneficial

Personal Attributes

Passionate about data quality, integrity and enabling data‑driven decision‑making
Strong analytical, problem‑solving and attention‑to‑detail skills
A clear, inclusive communicator who values collaboration and customer outcomes
Proactive mindset with a continuous improvement and automation‑first approach
Comfortable working in environments undergoing change and system evolution

As the Principal Business Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the Major Projects unit through the Department’s transition to SAP S/4HANA.

You will lead the detailed gathering, validation and documentation of business requirements, including process flows, use cases, data maps, gap analyses and end‑to‑end business solutions.

You will work closely with business stakeholders, subject matter experts and the systems project team to ensure the proposed SAP solution aligns with internal business processes, financial requirements and operational needs.

You will also maintain and update business process documentation and user guidance to reflect new or updated workflows resulting from the system implementation.

Previous experience as a Business Analyst, ideally at Senior / Lead / Principal level
Experience with enterprise accounting and financial management systems, ideally SAP and S/4 HANA
Advanced MS Excel skills such as development, enhancement and deployment of reporting tools
Demonstrated experience in data analysis and data management, including data cleansing, migration, testing and issue resolution

Why Apply:

Work on a business-critical AML transformation program

High level of ownership and visibility across stakeholders

Competitive day rate and flexible contract environment

Overview
We are seeking an exceptional FP&A Analyst to join a high‑performing Financial Planning & Reporting team and play a key role in driving strategic, data‑led decision making across a complex organisation.
This role offers exposure to senior stakeholders, long‑term financial planning, and meaningful analysis that directly supports organisational outcomes.

What You’ll Do

Partner with business units to analyse financial performance, identify risks and opportunities, and deliver clear, insight‑driven commentary
Deliver monthly management reporting and support long‑term financial planning activities
Build, maintain and enhance complex financial models (OLAP experience highly regarded)
Design and manage Power BI dashboards that translate data into compelling financial stories
Lead process improvements and uplift financial capability across senior stakeholders
Support strategic and operational initiatives through deep‑dive financial analysis
What We’re Looking For

CA or CPA qualified finance professional
Strong FP&A experience with a business partnering focus
Advanced analytical skills with the ability to turn data into actionable insights
Strong Power BI capability and exposure to enterprise‑level financial modelling

If you are interested in this role, please apply now!

About us 
We are an award winning, national $16B public offer industry fund focused on the education and community sectors. Working for NGS Super means being part of something bigger and working to make a difference to our members and their financial future.  

Our people are key to our success, and as we expand, we’re committed to finding and retaining the right talent to take on the journey.  As well as a flexible and fun workplace, we offer competitive benefits including additional leave entitlements, personal & professional development and health & wellbeing programs. 

Click on the video below to learn more! 

The Role 
The Senior Manager, Asset Allocation leads the design and ongoing evolution of NGS Super’s strategic, dynamic and tactical asset allocation frameworks across all investment options. The role is responsible for developing SAA, overseeing DAA/TAA overlays, and advancing systematic and discretionary strategies to generate alpha, manage risk and enable efficient portfolio rebalancing. As a senior member of the Investments team, the role leads quantitative research, contributes to SAA and DAA reviews, synthesises external adviser insights, and produces high quality investment and economic research, with a strong focus on derivatives, risk overlays and advanced financial modelling.

What you’ll do
Asset allocation & overlays

Lead end to end SAA design across options, including objectives, constraints, ranges and rebalancing.
Own and evolve DAA/TAA and risk overlay frameworks (hedging, downside protection, quantitative strategies).
Build and maintain portfolio construction models, tools, reporting and dashboards for Investment leadership.

Research & market analysis

Deliver high quality quantitative research and clear investment recommendations.
Provide timely macro and cross asset market analysis to inform positioning.
Review external advice and stay across academic and industry developments.

Strategy sourcing & manager engagement

Identify and assess internal and external strategies to enhance alpha, resilience and liquidity.
Lead manager due diligence in partnership with Governance, Operations and Legal.
Negotiate commercial terms in line with APRA standards and NGS policy.

Monitoring & governance

Conduct ongoing strategy and manager reviews covering performance, risk, ESG and governance.
Maintain robust documentation, research artefacts and analysis for investment committees.
Recommend actions or changes where objectives are not being met.

About you 
To succeed in this instrumental role, you will bring the following key skills:

5+ years’ experience in asset or portfolio management, with a strong interest in superannuation and investment analysis. 
Relevant finance qualifications (e.g. CFA, Actuarial, B.Com, B.Acc, B.Econ). 
Portfolio management experience with strong understanding of equity manager styles and company valuation. 
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with investment managers. 
Advanced analytical capability, combining qualitative judgement with strong Excel skills. 
Experience negotiating commercial terms with managers and service providers, in partnership with Legal. 
Intellectual curiosity, sound judgement and the maturity to represent the Fund with confidence.

The following skills are nice to have but not essential:

Experience using portfolio and market data tools (e.g. Bloomberg). 
Programming capability in Python, VBA or similar, with working knowledge of SQL. 
Understanding of derivatives and portfolio implementation. 
Experience managing outsourced providers and service delivery. 
Exposure to advanced investment techniques, including machine learning or market microstructure. 
Willingness to travel and work across time zones.

If you’re an experienced investment professional looking to shape portfolio outcomes and influence strategic asset allocation, we’d love to hear from you.

We are a super fund that has an exceptional work culture, provides a diverse offering in developing our people and you can be a part of it while earning an attractive remuneration package! 

Interested and suitably qualified candidates should submit a copy of their updated CV. 

Please note that to be eligible for this role, you are required to have permanent Australian working rights and residency. 

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a workplace that values diversity, equity, and respect for all individuals.

AI Change & Adoption Manager
Drive Enterprise Claude Adoption

Sydney / Contract / Hybrid 

Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Here's the truth about enterprise AI rollouts: the technology works. The people problem is what kills them.

Companies are deploying Claude and Cowork across their organisations, and the technology is genuinely transformative. But if employees don't adopt it, if managers resist it, if teams don't understand how it changes their workflows,  the project fails. Every time.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. We deliver end-to-end Claude deployments for enterprise clients, and we need Change & Adoption Managers who understand that technology transformation is really a people transformation.

What you'll do on client engagements
Design and execute adoption strategies for Claude and Cowork deployments across organisations of 500–10,000+ employees
Run hands-on workshops showing teams how AI changes their specific workflows — not generic AI demos, but "here's how Claude handles your actual invoice processing"
Build adoption measurement frameworks — tracking usage, sentiment, productivity impact, and resistance patterns
Coach executives and middle management on leading through AI-driven change — the layer where adoption most often stalls
Create communication plans that address the real fears (job displacement, skill obsolescence) honestly and constructively
Work with Integration Engineers and Config Specialists to ensure the technology experience matches the change narrative
What you bring
5+ years in change management, digital transformation, or organisational development
Experience leading adoption programs for enterprise technology rollouts — ERP, CRM, Office, collaboration platforms, or similar
Strong facilitation and workshop skills — you can hold a room of skeptical employees and bring them along
Data-driven approach to measuring adoption — you track metrics, not just vibes
Understanding that AI adoption is different from traditional IT rollouts — the emotional and identity dimensions are bigger
Bonus (not required)
Prosci, ADKAR, or similar change management certification
Experience with AI or automation adoption specifically
Background in L&D, instructional design, or training program development
Experience in regulated industries where change moves slowly and stakeholder management is critical
What Galileo offers
Great contract rates depending on experience
Free Anthropic certification — you'll understand the technology deeply enough to credibly lead adoption
A delivery team around you — architects design the solution, engineers build it, you make sure humans actually use it
Growing client pipeline across industries that are all hitting the same adoption challenges
Why this role matters more than any other on the team:
An Integration Engineer can build a perfect Claude deployment. A Solutions Architect can design flawless architecture. But if 4,000 employees ignore it and keep using their old workflows, the entire engagement was a waste of money. You're the person who makes the investment pay off.

If you've ever watched a brilliant technology project fail because nobody managed the human side — this is your chance to fix that at scale, with the most transformative technology since the internet.

To apply: Share your CV and a brief example of an adoption program you led — what worked, what didn't, and what you'd do differently. We value honesty over perfection.

Company: Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) is looking for an experienced Data Engineer with a proven track record in designing, building and maintaining core data platforms and pipelines that will underpin regulatory insight, intelligence and decision-making.

Reporting to the Senior Manager Data Management and Platform, this role will focus on ensuring that the data collected, integrated and stored by ONRSR is reliable, secure, scalable and fit-for-purpose in the safety regulation context. In this newly formed role, you will have the opportunity to meaningfully contribute to a new, strategic operating model where data is key to regulatory decisions and outcomes. With strong relationships across the Safety and Regulatory Insights team, as well as other stakeholders, you will effectively translate regulatory and operational requirements into robust technical solutions that enable evidence-based regulation and safety improvement.

As the only organisation in Australia with a complete view of national rail safety performance, ONRSR is uniquely placed to provide safety insights. Leveraging your strengths and unique skillset, combined with your values-led approach to working and producing outcomes, the Data Engineer is responsible for ensuring reliability, availability and performance of data platforms supporting the enterprise data warehouse and advanced analytics use cases. You will apply a continuous improvement mindset, optimising data structures and performance to support analytics, reporting and intelligence.

If you are passionate about the transformative ability of data, information and insights while looking to have an impact on rail safety, this may be the role for you.

About you – your impact and contribution

In this role, you will translate complex regulatory and operational requirements into practical, scalable data solutions within a modern Microsoft Fabric environment, while taking ownership of data pipelines and platform components to ensure reliability, performance and maintainability. You will work closely with analysts and stakeholders to deliver data that is usable, trusted and well understood, leveraging Fabric capabilities to enable seamless data integration, modelling and access. You will also contribute to improving how data is structured, governed and consumed across the organisation, with a continuous focus on simplifying, optimising and modernising the data ecosystem.

Key to success in this role includes:  

Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.  
Drive for, and skills to, building meaningful and effective relationships across the organisation.  
A commitment to continuous improvement, including own professional development as well as ways of working, contributing and producing outcomes for the organisation.  
Demonstrated experience in data engineering, including data pipelines, databases and integration technologies.  
A constructive approach to design solutions in complex, multi-source data environments.  
Understanding the importance of information security, privacy and risk considerations in government or regulated settings, including relevant governance obligations.
Experience using Apache Parquet and MS Fabric, as well as advanced knowledge and experience of SQL and working with heavily normalised data structures.
A strategic thinking mindset, combined with a strong ability to prioritise competing demands through understanding the role of data in various analytical models.    

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes.

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

The application process

Not sure you meet all the requirements? That’s okay — we’re interested in people who may not tick all the boxes. If this sounds like a place you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. 

To Apply, please submit your resume along with a cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for.

More information can be found in the Position Description which is attached below and can also be found at: https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

As part of the recruitment and selection process, shortlisted candidates will be required to undertake a national criminal history check. Please note that the outcome of this check will be considered in context and does not automatically disqualify applicants from progressing.

Enquiries to: ONRSR’s People and Capability team by emailing recruitment@onrsr.com.au

Salary: $142,416 - $168,216 + 12% super

Applications close: Wednesday, 22 April 2026, 6:00PM

At ONRSR, we’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, including those of diverse gender identities, sexual orientations, cultures, religions, ages, abilities and neurodiversity.

If there’s anything we can do to make the recruitment process more accessible or comfortable for you, please let us know – we’re here to support you.

We also commit to responding to every applicant.

NOTE:

You must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident or hold a valid work permit or visa, as we are unable to provide sponsorship.
Applications are sought from direct candidates only. No agencies please.
Application closing dates are at the discretion of ONRSR and may change without notice.

Position Description - Data Engineer

Location: Brisbane, QLD

Salary Package: $130-$150K + Super & Bonus DOE

Role highlights: Strategic Pricing Manager role in a global business



Role Overview

Exclusive to Reo Group, this is a highly exciting role to provide ongoing support to the Head of Pricing and key stakeholders within a global Retailer. This role is being recruited to optimise growth and commercial opportunity and works within an entrepreneurial environment whereby positive change and transformation are the norm.

Where the client is looking to achieve value in this hire is through acquiring a strategic individual who has demonstrated prior work in driving business growth, specifically around pricing, innovation, and gaining stakeholder trust. The BAU aspect to this role is also very varied and requires a candidate who is happy to work with a high degree of autonomy. Key technical requirements involve prior use of Snowflake and either Power BI / Advanced MS Excel.



Responsibilities

Design, test and implement Pricing and Promotional strategies that balance volume with profitability
Monitor food and labour costs as well as competitor pricing and demand trends
Partner with senior Marketing stakeholders to design and optimise promotional campaigns.
Conduct cost-benefit analysis to ensure promotions are commercially viable and aligned with brand positioning
Ensure pricing strategies are executable across various channels
Develop reports and dashboards to monitor pricing impact alongside margin and benefit realisation



Candidate Profile

This role is a brilliant opportunity for a candidate with solid exposure to strategic pricing initiatives, the ability to provide first class business partnering expertise and someone who looks to bring clarity in a positively changing business. The client needs someone who is invested in building a career in a business which is fast paced and where you will be required to demonstrate flexibility and adaptability to ever changing scenarios.

The ideal candidate will ideally be experienced within a B2C organisation with strong communication skills and the ability to utilise tools such as SQL, Snowflake and Power BI / MS Excel. The role could provide an opportunity for a candidate to step-up from a Senior Pricing Analyst role or step across from an existing Pricing Manager position.



Company, Culture & Benefits

The business is an innovative global giant and this role will be part of a team that supports business growth. This organisation is known for being people focused and prides itself on being committed to serving the consumer products industry across the globe. A hybrid working pattern is available for this role, with the team being office based for 4 days per week.



Pricing Manager role to drive strategy and change
Be rewarded: $130-$150k + Super & Bonus DOE
Opportunity to support acceleration of Business change



Apply

Company: Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity 

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) is looking for an experienced Senior Business Intelligence Analyst to drive and enable our Safety Insights and Regulatory Intelligence capability through the design, development and maintenance of high-quality insights and analytics. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Advanced Analytics and Insights in this newly formed role, you will be a key contributor to a refined, strategic operating model where transforming data into meaningful insights, intelligence and visual analytics products will play an integral role in supporting regulatory decision-making. 

Collaborating with stakeholders across the organisation, you will elicit business requirements to effectively translate them into clear, reliable and decision-ready insights and intelligence solutions for executives, regulators and operational teams. Your passion for meaningful data insights will drive you to create trusted, consistent and accessible business intelligence products that will assist in risk-based regulatory planning, monitoring of safety performance and regulatory outcomes, as well as the transparency and defensibility of regulatory decisions.  

If you are an experienced business intelligence professional, looking to make a positive impact in a purpose-driven and values-based organisation that is focused on improving community safety, this may be the opportunity for you. 

About you – your impact and contribution

We are seeking a motivated, diligent and experienced Senior Business Intelligence Analyst to play a pivotal role in advancing our regulatory intelligence capability. You will contribute to building a modern insights-driven regulatory environment where data is transformed into clear defensible and actionable insights that informs national safety decisions.  

To be successful in the role, you will be a proactive team player, collaborating with a wide range of stakeholders, and driven by producing meaningful business intelligence services and outcomes. You will have proven experience in designing, developing and maintaining high quality and impactful dashboards, reports and visualisations that provide deducible insights for their intended audiences. Your deep technical expertise will support the application of best practice data visualisation, presenting complex information clearly and accurately. 

As the Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, interpersonal skills are essential, leveraging expert communication skills, rapport and relationship-building as well as coaching skills to support the seamless integration of data services across the organisation as well as data literacy uplift. 

The ideal candidate for this role will have: 

Demonstrated analytical, and problem-solving skills, including a well-developed ability to translate complex data into recommendations and commentary to inform decision making.
Proven experience in defining semantic layers, metrics, dimensions and business rules.
Certification in Microsoft Power BI, MS Fabric or other relevant BI certifications.
Proficiency in SQL and working with structured data models in data warehouses.
Deep understanding of the existing and emerging tools and technology in the field.
Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.
A commitment to continuous improvement and growth, including own professional development as well as the ability to coach and support other team members.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and integrity.
A collaborative mindset paired with the ability to work within multidisciplinary teams.
Demonstrated experience in understanding and applying data governance principles, including data standards, metadata, lineage and stewardship.
Experience working with sensitive or regulated data, including ensuring its integrity and appropriate use. 

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People 

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes. 

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

The application process

Not sure you meet all the requirements? That’s okay — we’re interested in people who may not tick all the boxes. If this sounds like a place you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. 

To Apply, please submit your resume along with a cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for.

More information can be found in the Position Description which is attached below and can also be found at: https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

As part of the recruitment and selection process, shortlisted candidates will be required to undertake a national criminal history check. Please note that the outcome of this check will be considered in context and does not automatically disqualify applicants from progressing.

Enquiries to: ONRSR’s People and Capability team by emailing recruitment@onrsr.com.au

Salary: $135,192 – $164,534 + 12% super

Applications close: Wednesday, 22 April 2026, 6:00PM

At ONRSR, we’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, including those of diverse gender identities, sexual orientations, cultures, religions, ages, abilities and neurodiversity.

If there’s anything we can do to make the recruitment process more accessible or comfortable for you, please let us know – we’re here to support you.

We also commit to responding to every applicant.

NOTE:

You must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident or hold a valid work permit or visa, as we are unable to provide sponsorship.
Applications are sought from direct candidates only. No agencies please.
Application closing dates are at the discretion of ONRSR and may change without notice.

Position Description - Senior Business Intelligence Analyst

About the role – Multiple roles (Permanent & 12-month fixed term contract)

Calling for Payment System integration and Digital Banking end to end solution delivery experience for Sr. Business analyst roles.

It’s an exciting time to be a Senior Business Analyst at People First Bank!  Our team of Business Analysts are at the forefront of shaping the creation of a new bank by working on exciting and complex projects.

We are now offering a unique opportunity for an outcome-driven Senior Business Analyst with strong technical experience across the Payments System integration & Compliance projects, Financial Crimes working in a cross-functional team to deliver digital banking solutions and supporting problem solving activities that promote customer centric intuitive solutions. You will collaborate with multiple stakeholders to provide end-to-end ownership of requirements and user stories whilst coordinating and supporting problem solving activities that promote customer centric intuitive solutions. You will work collaboratively with business representatives, delivery lead, product owners, and technology leads to add value and drive outcomes across the business. Furthermore, as a Business Analyst, you will actively contribute to the Community of Practice maturity across the team, helping to shape best practices and guide team members.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Lead payments domain analysis, partner with Product Owners and Delivery Leads to ensure solutions deliver measurable customer and business outcomes

·        Collaborating with design and frontline teams to understand problems our customers are experiencing, apply design thinking principles to create user-centric and effective solutions.

·        Lead end-to-end discovery and delivery for complex initiatives, partnering with stakeholders to shape, define, and deliver solutions aligned to business strategy and technical feasibility.

·        Own delivery scoping and requirements definition for complex initiatives, leading story development, customer journeys, and process changes to clearly articulate outcomes, impacts, and rationale for change.

·        Own backlog quality through effective refinement, testable acceptance criteria, and alignment to delivery priorities, and readiness for delivery.

·        Proactively identify, assess, and manage risks, dependencies, and cross‑functional impacts, providing clear recommendations to support decision‑making across business and technology.

·        Support and influence key delivery decisions by evaluating options, trade‑offs, regulatory impacts, and technical constraints.

·        Champion legal and compliance obligations requirements applicable, where required engage with business owner to ensure compliance is met.

·        Key driver to the planning and support business readiness activities.

·        Define requirements with the business with designing reporting and dashboards that will feed to downstream data and technology teams.

·        Proactively manage stakeholder alignment and resolve competing priorities across business and technology.

·        Define functional and non-functional requirements, ensuring regulatory traceability and downstream data impacts are considered.

·        Support change, operational readiness, and smooth transition of solutions into BAU

·        Be an active member of the Business Analyst practice to drive improvements in the Business Analyst capability

·        Support the design, implementation, and embedding of development programs for BA cohort.

·        Actively promote continuous improvements to ways of working across the business.




 About you 

You are a Senior Business Analyst with solid experience delivering payments related initiatives. Adept at bridging the gap between business needs and technology solutions through effective stakeholder engagement, structured analysis and a collaborative approach. You enjoy guiding and sharing knowledge with others and work well with a large team to achieve great outcomes for our customers.

To be successful in the role, you must have the following skills:

·        A seasoned BA with 8+ experience in all facets of business analysis.

·        Strong knowledge of digital banking products and services, such as onboarding, account origination, payments, lending and digital channels

·        Strong confidence in leading payments and cards solution, technical experience to derive requirements from frontend digital banking solutions to backend systems including payment orchestration layer APIs (e.g. payment gateways, processors, acquirers)

·        Applying your knowledge of Australian banking regulations to satisfy our regulatory obligations within Australian Payments, Cards and Fraud compliance including payment and card scheme requirements

·        The ability to break down complex problems and identify solutions

·        Understanding of API's and experience in creating high level solution designs

·        Experience with the Backbase Digital Banking Platform is highly regarded.

·        In-depth understanding of IT Risk frameworks.




Additionally, it’s essential that you have experience with the following:

Financial services industry experience 

Technical interface specification development

Customer centric product development

Process analysis and documentation

Experience working with customer relationship management software, especially Salesforce CRM, would be highly regarded

Be an engaging communicator, comfortable negotiating and influencing your key stakeholder management group.

Adaptable and capable in dealing with complexities and ambiguity in a constantly evolving environment.

Experience managing multiple activities and issues across numerous projects in a high paced environment.

Experience in requirements management, utilizing Agile methodologies and tools including Jira and Confluence. 

Enjoys leading and coaching other BAs.

Certification such as BABOK, Lean Six Sigma

 

Location

Whilst our primary office locations are across Adelaide, Brisbane and Toowoomba, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 

A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

Company: Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity 

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) is looking for an experienced Senior Business Intelligence Analyst to drive and enable our Safety Insights and Regulatory Intelligence capability through the design, development and maintenance of high-quality insights and analytics. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Advanced Analytics and Insights in this newly formed role, you will be a key contributor to a refined, strategic operating model where transforming data into meaningful insights, intelligence and visual analytics products will play an integral role in supporting regulatory decision-making. 

Collaborating with stakeholders across the organisation, you will elicit business requirements to effectively translate them into clear, reliable and decision-ready insights and intelligence solutions for executives, regulators and operational teams. Your passion for meaningful data insights will drive you to create trusted, consistent and accessible business intelligence products that will assist in risk-based regulatory planning, monitoring of safety performance and regulatory outcomes, as well as the transparency and defensibility of regulatory decisions.  

If you are an experienced business intelligence professional, looking to make a positive impact in a purpose-driven and values-based organisation that is focused on improving community safety, this may be the opportunity for you. 

About you – your impact and contribution

We are seeking a motivated, diligent and experienced Senior Business Intelligence Analyst to play a pivotal role in advancing our regulatory intelligence capability. You will contribute to building a modern insights-driven regulatory environment where data is transformed into clear defensible and actionable insights that informs national safety decisions.  

To be successful in the role, you will be a proactive team player, collaborating with a wide range of stakeholders, and driven by producing meaningful business intelligence services and outcomes. You will have proven experience in designing, developing and maintaining high quality and impactful dashboards, reports and visualisations that provide deducible insights for their intended audiences. Your deep technical expertise will support the application of best practice data visualisation, presenting complex information clearly and accurately. 

As the Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, interpersonal skills are essential, leveraging expert communication skills, rapport and relationship-building as well as coaching skills to support the seamless integration of data services across the organisation as well as data literacy uplift. 

The ideal candidate for this role will have: 

Demonstrated analytical, and problem-solving skills, including a well-developed ability to translate complex data into recommendations and commentary to inform decision making.
Proven experience in defining semantic layers, metrics, dimensions and business rules.
Certification in Microsoft Power BI, MS Fabric or other relevant BI certifications.
Proficiency in SQL and working with structured data models in data warehouses.
Deep understanding of the existing and emerging tools and technology in the field.
Excellent communication and facilitation skills, demonstrating the capacity to effectively connect and communicate with a range of technical and non-technical stakeholders.
A commitment to continuous improvement and growth, including own professional development as well as the ability to coach and support other team members.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and integrity.
A collaborative mindset paired with the ability to work within multidisciplinary teams.
Demonstrated experience in understanding and applying data governance principles, including data standards, metadata, lineage and stewardship.
Experience working with sensitive or regulated data, including ensuring its integrity and appropriate use. 

Our Organisation and Culture

The Office of the National Rail Safety Regulator (ONRSR) has the responsibility for regulatory oversight of rail safety throughout Australia. As an independent regulatory authority, ONRSR pursues safe railways for Australia by encouraging safe rail operations, enforcing national compliance with the Rail Safety National Law and promoting and improving rail safety across the country.

Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong commitment to its Culture Blueprint - the foundation for our purposeful, diverse and inclusive workplace and a key contributor to how we achieve our vision of Safe Railways for all Australians.

We are passionate about supporting Our People 

At ONRSR we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for Rail Safety for all Australians. Our colleagues have an impact far beyond work, and you’ll be part of a team that is contributing to enhancing safety outcomes. 

You will be part of a $26 billion industry employing over 142,000 people that is at the forefront of innovation, productivity, and world’s best practice.
We invest in building the capability of our people through customised learning and development programs and by growing your curiosity and experience.
Employee Assistance Program and Peer Support Network.
Mentoring for your own development and the opportunity to mentor others.
Extra 3 days leave with Christmas closedown for all colleagues.
Salary packaging of car.

We support healthy work life balance, encourage ongoing learning and growth, and offer flexible work options to help our people succeed both professionally and personally. Whether you’re building new skills or deepening your expertise, you’ll find a team environment that’s focused on connection, contribution, and continuous improvement. Further details on the benefits of working at ONRSR can be found at https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

The application process

Not sure you meet all the requirements? That’s okay — we’re interested in people who may not tick all the boxes. If this sounds like a place you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. 

To Apply, please submit your resume along with a cover letter outlining why you are interested in the role and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for.

More information can be found in the Position Description which is attached below and can also be found at: https://www.onrsr.com.au/about-onrsr/working-at-onrsr

Ad Text:
Role Overview:

Our client is looking to appoint a Head of Finance Systems and Data Analytics, a critical leadership role within the finance function.
This position sits at the intersection of finance, technology and data, with accountability for acting as the functional design authority for the organisation’s cloud ERP platform, establishing and leading a finance data governance framework, and enabling high quality, insight driven decision making across the enterprise.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of finance systems and data capability within a complex, large-scale environment.

Key responsibilities

Finance systems ownership

Lead the configuration, governance and optimisation of the ERP platform
Oversee system integrations, workflows and release cycles
Partner with technology teams and vendors to ensure system performance and scalability
Data governance and stewardship

Establish and chair a finance data governance framework
Define data standards, ownership and quality controls
Ensure consistency across key data sets including financial master data
Analytics and reporting

Own the finance reporting and analytics strategy
Drive the transition to modern, self-service reporting capabilities
Deliver accurate and timely insights to support executive decision-making
Operational performance and compliance

Manage system health, service levels and incident resolution
Ensure compliance with audit, regulatory and data security requirements
Leadership and stakeholder engagement

Lead and develop a high-performing team
Partner with senior stakeholders across finance, technology and transformation
Manage key vendor and external delivery relationships
Key experience includes

Proven leadership in finance systems, ERP environments or transformation programmes
Strong understanding of cloud ERP platforms such as Oracle, SAP or similar
Experience in data governance and analytics enablement
Ability to translate complex data into meaningful business insights
Strong stakeholder management across executive and technical audiences
Experience delivering large-scale system or finance transformation initiatives
Why this role

Opportunity to lead a high impact transformation programme
Influence enterprise wide finance systems and data strategy
Join a purpose led organisation with a strong market presence
Work in a collaborative and forward thinking environment
How to Apply

Click the “Apply” button below. If additional information is required reach out to Josh Tinton at jtinton@quinnallan.com.au

As a Change Manager with Akkodis Australia, you help clients deliver IT and digital transformation that is human-centred, psychologically safe and measurable. You guide stakeholders through ambiguity, build alignment and trust, and design change experiences that support real adoption and the new ways of working the organisation needs. You adapt your approach to each delivery scenario, integrating change activities into project, program or agile delivery environments.

You bring curiosity, empathy and the ability to co-design with multiple groups. You enable teams to test, learn and evolve in short cycles so the solution and the human experience progress together.


What you will lead:

Human‑centered change design

Shape the HUMAN‑First change journey to support clarity, psychological safety, trust-building and inclusive engagement
Facilitate co-design workshops, empathy-based discovery and human-centered impact analysis.

Change delivery within IT project environments

Integrate change activities into IT delivery lifecycles including waterfall, hybrid and agile contexts.
Work closely with the project team, client SMEs and impacted users to align readiness with releases, technical cutovers and deployment.

Communication, learning and behavioural adoption

Create narratives, messaging and user-focused learning that simplify complex technical change.
Support leaders and champions to model behaviours, communicate consistently and remove adoption barriers.

Micro‑testing and iterative adaptation

Lead HUMAN‑First micro-tests and safe experiments to validate new behaviours and processes before full rollout.
Use evidence and feedback to adapt plans and reduce risk.

Measurement and insight

Define human-centred success measures including readiness, sentiment, confidence and behavioural indicators.
Bring insights to decision-makers to refine strategy, delivery sequencing and adoption support.


Responsible AI and Ethical Delivery

Akkodis' transformation and delivery ecosystem increasingly includes AI tools, AI-enabled workflows and automation. Responsible AI practices ensure these capabilities are safe, transparent, equitable and human‑centred. As a Change Manager, you will:

Embed Responsible AI governance

Apply Responsible AI principles outlined in Akkodis governance and advisory frameworks, including transparency, accountability, data minimisation, inclusion and ethical risk management.

Ensure human-centred AI adoption

Support organisations to introduce AI in ways that reinforce psychological safety, trust and human oversight.
Ensure AI assists and augments human decision-making rather than replacing judgement or accountability.

Protect quality, safety and compliance

Ensure human review and validation is mandatory for AI‑generated artefacts, analysis or communication.
Track and manage AI-related risks including misuse, overreliance, data leakage, quality issues and misalignment across teams.

Drive transparency and ethical engagement

Communicate clearly how AI tools are used, what boundaries exist and how outputs are validated.
Facilitate conversations about concerns, ethical implications and workforce impacts to build trust.

Support AI-integrated delivery models

Work within AI-enabled project governance frameworks that include AI-specific stage gates, RAID items and guardrail monitoring.
Partner with technical teams delivering AI solutions to ensure the people experience, risks and behavioural impacts are anticipated and managed

What you bring

Human‑centred mindset
Change management expertise
Responsible AI awareness
Adaptive delivery capability
Collaboration and facilitation
Consulting professionalism

Qualifications

Preferred degree in business, psychology, communications, IT or a related field.
Change management credentials such as Prosci, APMG, Lean Change, Agile Change Leadership or equivalent (desirable).
Technical literacy across digital, IT or cloud environments is beneficial (for example with Akkodis technology partners AWS or Microsoft ecosystem exposure).

Talented, committed, can-do individuals have always been the cornerstone of our dynamic organisation. If you have what it takes to thrive in this role and want to know more, please reach out to Rose at rose.hampton@akkodisgroup.com


Why Work for Us?

Joining Akkodis means being part of a dynamic community of engineers that's at the forefront of tech innovation. You'll have the opportunity to work with cutting-edge technologies:

Whether your interests lie in systems engineering, software development, data platforms or any other tech discipline, we have a role that will suit you. You'll get to collaborate with talented expert colleagues from around the world who share your enthusiasm and passion for technology. We offer a flexible and supportive work environment, and we're committed to helping you grow and achieve your career goals. If you want to make a real difference, Akkodis is the place for you.

Akkodis is a commercial brand under which both AKKA and Modis entities operate.

Position Description - Senior Business Intelligence Analyst

A thorough understanding of Dynamics 365 data structures - including Dataverse, D365 Finance & Operations, and Customer Engagement entity models - is essential. The role demands strong DevOps practice, including the design and maintenance of CI/CD pipelines that underpin reliable, automated delivery of data platform components.

Candidates with experience integrating legacy systems into modern cloud-native platforms and enabling data science and ML workloads within Fabric will be highly regarded

Key Responsibilities:

Microsoft Fabric Platform Engineering

Design and implement scalable Lakehouse and Warehouse solutions in Microsoft Fabric using the medallion architecture (Bronze / Silver / Gold).
Build and maintain data ingestion pipelines using Data Factory (Dataflows Gen2, Copy Activity, orchestration pipelines).
Develop PySpark and Spark SQL notebooks for large-scale data transformation and enrichment within Fabric Data Engineering workloads
Manage OneLake storage strategy including Delta Lake table optimisation, partitioning, and schema evolution.

Azure Data Services

Design and operate Azure Data Factory pipelines for data movement across on-premises, cloud, and SaaS sources.
Implement and manage Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 as a scalable, hierarchical data store integrated with Fabric OneLake.
Leverage Azure Databricks for advanced Spark-based data engineering, streaming workloads, and unified analytics.
Utilise Azure Synapse Analytics for distributed query processing and hybrid transactional/analytical workloads where applicable.
Configure Azure Service Bus, Event Hubs, and Logic Apps to support event-driven and real-time integration architectures.

DevOps & CI/CD

Design, implement, and maintain CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps for automated build, test, and deployment of data platform components.
Manage source control and branching strategies using Azure DevOps Repos and Git, enforcing peer review and code quality gates.
Automate infrastructure provisioning using Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) tools such as Bicep or Terraform for Azure resources.
Implement automated testing frameworks for data pipeline validation, schema conformance, and data quality assertions.
Manage Fabric deployment pipelines (Dev / Test / Prod) and integrate them with Azure DevOps release workflows.
Maintain Azure DevOps Boards for sprint planning, work item tracking, and release management aligned to Agile delivery practices

Dynamics 365 Data Structures & Integration

Apply deep knowledge of Dynamics 365 data architecture - including Dataverse entity model, relationships, and metadata - to inform data engineering design decisions.
Implement Synapse Link for Dataverse to replicate D365 entities into Fabric OneLake for downstream analytics and reporting.
Design integration patterns leveraging D365 OData APIs, virtual tables, dual-write, and event-based export mechanisms.
Work with Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (F&O) and Customer Engagement (CE) data structures to extract, transform, and load business-critical datasets.
Collaborate with D365 functional consultants and architects to ensure data contracts and entity mappings are accurate, governed, and maintained.

Governance & Data Quality

Configure and maintain Microsoft Purview for data cataloguing, automated lineage capture, classification, and sensitivity labelling across Fabric and Azure assets.
Implement data quality frameworks and enforce DLP policies aligned to organisational data governance and compliance standards.
Ensure all data assets are discoverable, documented, and compliant with relevant regulatory and security requirements.

Analytisc & Reporting

Develop and optimise Power BI semantic models, leveraging DirectLake mode for real-time Fabric-native analytics.
Author complex DAX measures and manage model performance, incremental refresh, and deployment pipelines.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to translate requirements into performant, governed, and user-friendly dashboards

Essential Requirements:
Experience & Platform

10+ years of professional data engineering experience in enterprise environments.
Demonstrated hands-on experience across the Microsoft Fabric platform including Lakehouse, Warehouse, Data Factory, Data Engineering (Spark), OneLake, and Power BI.
Proven experience with Azure data services including Azure Data Factory, ADLS Gen2, Azure Databricks, and Azure Synapse Analytics.
Strong proficiency in PySpark, Python, and SQL for large-scale data transformation and pipeline development.
Practical knowledge of Microsoft Purview for data governance, cataloguing, lineage, and sensitivity classification.

Dynamics 365

In-depth understanding of Dynamics 365 data structures including the Dataverse entity model, table relationships, option sets, and metadata.
Hands-on experience with D365 Finance & Operations and/or Customer Engagement data architecture and common data entities.
Practical experience implementing Synapse Link for Dataverse or equivalent D365 data export mechanisms for analytics workloads.
Ability to interpret D365 functional configuration and translate it into sound data engineering design

DevOps & Engineering Practice

Demonstrated experience designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps for data platform delivery.
Proficiency with Git-based source control, branching strategies, and code review workflows.
Familiarity with Infrastructure-as-Code (Bicep or Terraform) for automated Azure resource provisioning.
Experience with automated testing for data pipelines, including schema validation and data quality assertions.
Solid understanding of data modelling principles including star schema, slowly changing dimensions, and medallion architecture.
Strong communication skills - able to engage technical and non-technical stakeholders effectively across delivery teams.

Desirable Criteria

Legacy Systems Integration

Proven track record migrating or integrating on-premises or legacy ERP/CRM systems into cloud-native data platforms.
Experience with heterogeneous source systems including SQL Server, Oracle, SAP, flat file/SFTP ingestion, and REST APIs.
Ability to assess technical debt and design pragmatic lift-and-shift or re-architecture migration patterns

Data Science & ML Ops

Hands-on experience with Fabric Data Science workloads, MLflow experiment tracking, and model operationalisation.
Ability to prepare feature engineering pipelines and curated datasets to support AI and ML use cases

Certifications (any of the following valued)

DP-700: Implementing Data Engineering Solutions Using Microsoft Fabric
DP-600: Implementing Analytics Solutions Using Microsoft Fabric
DP-500: Designing and Implementing Enterprise-Scale Analytics Solutions
PL-300: Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst
SC-400: Microsoft Information Protection Administrator (Purview)
AZ-204 / AZ-400: Azure Developer or DevOps Engineer Expert
MB-700: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations Apps Solution Architect
